title: Lesson 23: File Paths (Relative vs Absolute Paths)

content.explanation:
📁 How Websites Find Files

When you add images, links, or scripts, your browser needs to know exactly where the file is located.

This is done using something called a file path.

🌐 Why This Matters

Without correct file paths:

  • Images won’t load
  • Links will break
  • Websites will fail in production

This is one of the most important real-world skills in web development.

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Types of File Paths

1. Relative Path (Most Important)

<img src="images/profile.jpg">

👉 Used when files are inside your project folder

2. Same Folder Path

<img src="profile.jpg">

3. Absolute Path (URL)

<img src="https://example.com/image.jpg">

🧠 Key Rule

👉 Relative paths are used in real projects (GitHub, Netlify, Vercel)

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
My Portfolio

Learning how files connect in real websites.

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Practice file paths

1. Add 3 images below the paragraph:
   - One using same folder path (profile.jpg)
   - One using folder path (images/photo.jpg)
   - One using full URL (https://via.placeholder.com/150)

2. Add alt text for each image

🔥 Understand how real websites load assets
mini_challenge.hint: 👉 Example: Mix all 3 types of paths

quiz_engine.question: Which type of file path is most commonly used in real web projects?
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 Relative paths are essential in real projects because they keep your site portable across servers like GitHub, Netlify, and Vercel.

takeaways: File paths tell the browser where to find files
Relative paths are used inside project folders
Incorrect paths break images and links
File structure is critical in real deployments

cheat_sheet.rule: Use relative paths in real projects
